title: OVERDRIVE merch-brand logo finishing: variant set, transparent PNGs, SVG vector source, palette + font kit
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the Upwork brief (cand_42, db_i 2618): brand name "OVERDRIVE" in all caps, scholarship-focused merch brand, "bold, modern, youthful, collegiate, merch-ready — more premium streetwear than charity/nonprofit," a clean scalable graduation-cap element, must work on social PFPs / hoodies / hats / website headers / stickers / watermarks, and the requested deliverable list: primary logo, stacked logo, icon/avatar version, black version, white version, transparent PNGs, SVG, AI/EPS vector source, PDF, color palette, font recommendations, usage guide. REASONABLY ENRICHED to make it a runnable Adobe-connector benchmark: the brief as written ("I need a finalized logo… design") implies authoring a mark from scratch, which is in the capability sheet's [X]/"no" bucket (text-to-image, bespoke type drawing, illustration-from-scratch are NOT available). So this benchmark is honestly scoped to the PRODUCTION / FINISHING half a freelancer actually runs in Adobe once a logo concept exists: the client supplies a rendered raster logo lockup, and the connector chain removes backgrounds, derives the icon/avatar + social PFP crops, generates the black/white/mono solid-fill variants, vectorizes to a clean SVG source, extracts a palette reference, and recommends fonts. NOT enriched into an unrelated deliverable: every output maps to a line item the client literally asked for. The originality/commercial-rights written confirmation the brief requests is a human/contractual statement, noted in the deliverable spec but not a connector step.
one_line_ask: Take the client's supplied OVERDRIVE raster logo lockup and productionize it into the full merch brand-kit deliverable set — transparent primary/stacked/icon/avatar PNGs, social-PFP crop, black/white/mono variants, a clean SVG vector source, an extracted palette reference, and font recommendations — chaining every step in Adobe.
full_brief: OVERDRIVE is a new scholarship-focused merchandise brand (education support via merch sales + scholarship-style campaigns); the look must read bold, modern, youthful, collegiate and merch-ready — premium streetwear, not charity/nonprofit — with the name shown as OVERDRIVE in all caps and a clean, scalable graduation-cap element (no government/university seal, lottery, or generic-education-clipart vibe). The client has approved a rendered raster logo lockup (primary horizontal version on a flat background). Your job, executed entirely with the Adobe connector, is to finish that concept into the production brand-kit deliverable set the client requested: (1) upload the supplied logo lockup into Adobe storage; (2) strip its background to a clean transparent primary logo PNG; (3) derive a tight-cropped icon/avatar from the cap mark and a square social-profile-picture crop sized for PFPs; (4) generate the merch-critical single-color variants — a full BLACK version and a full WHITE version (for dark/light garments, stickers, and watermarks) using subject selection + solid fills, plus one monochromatic brand-tint alt; (5) vectorize the transparent logo into a clean, scalable SVG source for hoodies/hats/large print; (6) produce a flat palette/swatch reference for the color-palette deliverable; and (7) recommend brand fonts that match the collegiate-streetwear tone. Hard exclusions per Adobe capability ground truth: no from-scratch logo generation, no AI object removal, no compositing, no upscaling — the supplied lockup is the source of truth and every deliverable is derived from it by chaining. The brief's requested AI/EPS source and multipage PDF, and the written originality/commercial-rights confirmation, are noted as human/format-export finishing items layered on top of the connector-produced SVG + PNG masters.

inputs.name: overdrive_logo_lockup.png
inputs.gen_prompt: Flat 2D vector-style brand logo lockup for a streetwear scholarship merch brand, the wordmark 'OVERDRIVE' in heavy bold all-caps condensed collegiate-athletic sans-serif, with a single clean minimal graduation cap (mortarboard with a short tassel) integrated above or beside the wordmark. Bold modern youthful premium-streetwear feel, NOT a government or university seal, NOT a lottery logo, NOT generic education clipart. Two-color: deep navy and a bright varsity gold. Rendered as a finished horizontal lockup centered on a plain flat light-gray studio background with even margins, crisp anti-aliased edges, no photographic texture, no drop shadow. High resolution, 2400x1400px.
inputs.realism_notes: Must look like a delivered raster logo export (flat, even, gallery-style on a plain solid background) so background removal and vectorization have clean edges to work on. Text 'OVERDRIVE' must be legible and correctly spelled in all caps — this is why gemini-3-pro-image (text/logo-faithful) is used rather than a photoreal model. Keep it strictly two-color with hard edges; avoid gradients, glow, or 3D bevels that would muddy the vectorize step.

outputs.name: OVERDRIVE_primary_logo_transparent.png
outputs.spec: Primary horizontal lockup, background removed, RGBA transparent, full source resolution (~2400x1400).

outputs.name: OVERDRIVE_icon_avatar_transparent.png
outputs.spec: Compact icon/avatar version of the mark: the transparent primary lockup tight-cropped to its content bounds (removes the surrounding empty margin so the whole wordmark+cap lockup sits edge-to-edge as a dense badge). Transparent RGBA. NOTE: this is a tight-cropped full lockup, not a cap-only cutout — a headless content-bounds crop cannot isolate the graduation cap alone from a horizontal wordmark lockup.

outputs.name: OVERDRIVE_social_pfp_1080.png
outputs.spec: Square social profile-picture asset derived from the transparent icon via image_crop_and_resize to 1080x1080, transparent RGBA. The icon is fit centered into the square frame with transparent margins on the short axis (honest reframe/letterbox of an already-transparent asset — no background is generated or filled and no source canvas is extended).

outputs.name: OVERDRIVE_logo_black.png
outputs.spec: Full BLACK (solid #000000) single-color version of the lockup on transparency — for light garments, stickers, watermarks.

outputs.name: OVERDRIVE_logo_white.png
outputs.spec: Full WHITE (solid #FFFFFF) single-color version of the lockup on transparency — for dark garments and headers.

outputs.name: OVERDRIVE_logo_mono_navy.png
outputs.spec: Monochromatic navy-tinted single-color brand alt of the lockup.

outputs.name: OVERDRIVE_logo_source.svg
outputs.spec: Clean vectorized SVG source of the transparent primary logo — scalable master for hoodies/hats/large print (stands in for the AI/EPS source deliverable).

outputs.name: OVERDRIVE_palette_swatches.png
outputs.spec: Flat two-tone color-palette reference built honestly from real regions of the cropped icon canvas: the transparent background field is solid-filled with deep brand navy and the mark's own pixels are solid-filled with varsity gold, yielding a navy+gold color-field reference tile for the color-palette deliverable (the accompanying font/spec kit documents exact hex values). NOTE: the fills recolor regions that actually exist on the canvas (background vs. subject) — no fabricated pre-existing swatch blocks are selected.

outputs.name: OVERDRIVE_font_recommendations.json
outputs.spec: Recommended brand font set (collegiate-streetwear display + supporting text) with names/rationale.

connector_workflow.note: Begin upload of the client's supplied raster logo lockup into Adobe storage; returns an upload target/session.
Finalize the logo upload; yields the stored logo asset URN/URL used as the source of truth for the whole chain.
Strip the flat background -> clean transparent PRIMARY logo PNG. Deliverable: OVERDRIVE_primary_logo_transparent.png.

Tight-crop the transparent logo to its content bounds -> compact icon/avatar version (the whole wordmark+cap lockup with the empty margin trimmed off, edge-to-edge). This is a content-bounds crop of the FULL lockup, not a cap-only isolate — a headless bounds crop cannot separate just the graduation cap from a horizontal wordmark lockup. Deliverable: OVERDRIVE_icon_avatar_transparent.png.

Reframe/resize the transparent icon to a 1080x1080 square social profile-picture asset, fitting the mark centered with transparent margins on the short axis (honest letterbox of an already-transparent asset — no background fill, no canvas extension). Deliverable: OVERDRIVE_social_pfp_1080.png.
Select the logo marks (wordmark + cap) on the transparent primary to drive single-color fills.
Solid-fill the selected marks with #000000 -> full BLACK version (solid color only, not generative). Deliverable: OVERDRIVE_logo_black.png.
Re-fill the same selected marks with #FFFFFF -> full WHITE version on transparency. Deliverable: OVERDRIVE_logo_white.png.
Apply a single-hue navy monochromatic tint -> brand mono alt. Deliverable: OVERDRIVE_logo_mono_navy.png.
Vectorize the clean transparent primary into a scalable SVG source master (hoodies/hats/large print). Deliverable: OVERDRIVE_logo_source.svg.

Palette build step 1: select the mark (the actual wordmark+cap pixels) on the cropped icon canvas. This selects a region that genuinely exists on the canvas — subject vs. transparent background — rather than fabricating swatch blocks.
Palette build step 2: invert the subject selection to get the surrounding (background) region as a mask, so the two real regions of the canvas can each take one solid brand color.
Palette build step 3: solid-fill the background region with deep brand navy. image_fill_area lays exactly ONE solid color into ONE selected region (solid fill only, not generative).

Palette build step 4: solid-fill the mark region (the reused subject mask from step 11) with varsity gold, yielding the flat navy+gold two-tone color-field reference. Deliverable: OVERDRIVE_palette_swatches.png.
Recommend collegiate-streetwear display + supporting text fonts matched to the lockup. Deliverable: OVERDRIVE_font_recommendations.json.

chaining_note: Strict output->input chain. Steps 1->2 upload the client logo -> logo_asset_urn. Step 3 (remove_background) consumes logo_asset_urn -> primary_logo_transparent, the hub master. Step 4 content-bounds crops it -> icon_avatar_transparent (a tight-cropped FULL lockup used as the compact icon/avatar, not a cap-only isolate — a bounds crop cannot separate just the cap from a horizontal wordmark lockup); step 5 resizes that icon -> social_pfp_1080. Step 6 selects the marks on primary_logo_transparent -> logo_mark_mask; step 7 fills that mask black (consuming primary_logo_transparent + the mask) -> logo_black; step 8 re-fills the SAME mask on logo_black white -> logo_white. Step 9 tints primary_logo_transparent -> logo_mono_navy. Step 10 vectorizes primary_logo_transparent -> logo_source_svg. Steps 11-14 build the palette swatch reference by recoloring the two real regions of the cropped icon canvas: step 11 selects the subject (the actual mark pixels) -> palette_subject_mask; step 12 inverts that to the background region -> palette_background_mask; step 13 solid-fills the background navy -> palette_build_navy; step 14 solid-fills the mark region (reusing palette_subject_mask) gold -> palette_swatches; each image_fill_area lays exactly ONE solid color into ONE genuinely-present region (no fabricated swatch blocks are prompt-selected). Step 15 recommends fonts from primary_logo_transparent. Every post-load step consumes at least one prior step's named output; the delivered kit is the union of the produced PNG/SVG/JSON masters.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon (15 steps, 11 distinct Adobe tools, well past the ≥10 calls / ≥6 tools bar) and a genuine wide mix across A1 raster (remove_background, select_subject, invert_selection, fill_area×4, monochromatic_tint, crop_to_bounds, crop_and_resize, vectorize), A2 document/typography (font_recommend), and A4 asset upload (init/finalize upload). It is a real freelancer production job: derive an entire merch brand-kit deliverable set from one approved logo lockup. Difficulty comes from disciplined masking-and-fill reuse (one subject selection drives two single-color logo variants, and a select+invert pair drives the two-tone palette tile), correct ordering (background must be removed before crop/vectorize so edges are clean), and staying strictly inside Adobe ground truth — the brief's tempting from-scratch-logo and AI-removal asks are explicitly out of scope, so the spec is honestly scoped to the productionizable half and notes the AI/EPS+PDF+rights-letter as human finishing items. Every step is connector-headless [C].

reverify.banned_capability_check: No banned capability used. Every step is a real Adobe connector action: uploads (asset_initialize/finalize_file_upload), image_remove_background (native BG strip, not AI object removal), crop_to_bounds/crop_and_resize (gen-free reframe/crop, no canvas growth or extension — the icon is an honest content-bounds tight-crop of the full lockup, not a fabricated cap isolate and not a padded/enlarged canvas), image_select_subject/image_invert_selection (mask-only, non-generative), image_fill_area (SOLID single-color fill of ONE selected region per call -- logo B/W variants via one reused subject mask, and the palette tile via a select-subject + invert pair recoloring the two REAL regions of the icon canvas, no multi-color single-call over-claim and no fabricated pre-existing swatch blocks), image_apply_monochromatic_tint, image_vectorize (raster->SVG trace, not from-scratch art), font_recommend. No text-to-image, no from-scratch logo/art, no outpaint, no canvas extension/padding, no upscale/super-resolution, no compositing, no object-removal-with-reconstruction, no mask fed to lens_blur, no preview-into-new-file. All workflow inputs are declared connector outputs or the declared source input. full_brief, outputs[].spec, and chaining_note contain no banned-capability phrasing.
reverify.notes: Fixed all flagged findings: (1) the icon/avatar deliverable + crop step note honestly describe a content-bounds tight-crop of the FULL wordmark+cap lockup (removed the impossible 'cap-mark isolated from the lockup' claim, since a headless bounds crop cannot separate the cap alone); (2) the palette sub-chain is an honest select-subject -> invert-selection -> two solid fills sequence that recolors the two genuinely-present regions (mark vs. background) of the icon canvas into a navy+gold color-field reference. Trimmed the review-only Firefly usage moodboard and the redundant re-bundling brand_kit.zip (plus the hoodie-mock upload steps and hoodie input that fed only the board); structured counts reconciled to tool_call_count=15, distinct_adobe_tools=11, tools_used dropped create_firefly_board; difficulty_rationale and chaining_note renumbered to match. Task stays real, coherent, and fully Adobe-connector-doable.
